topic: project-memory-hook-detection
finding: mkdir flagged as research tool because isExploratoryBash() in pre-tool-use.js uses two-layer detection: Layer 1 checks description keywords (exploration vs operational), Layer 2 checks command regex. If description scoring is ambiguous or missing, the fallback may misclassify operational commands.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: hook-intent-fix
finding: Fixed both hooks: post-tool-use.js now has isExploratoryBash() and isExploratoryTask() to skip save reminders for operational tools. pre-tool-use.js GATE 2 now uses isExploratoryTask() so operational Task subagents (code-simplifier, gsd-executor) skip memory check and escalation gates.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Hook intent detection: false positive root cause and fix for Bash/Task classification
finding: post-tool-use.js was flagging ALL Bash commands (mkdir, npm install, git push) as research tools because it never called isExploratoryBash() — the function only existed in pre-tool-use.js. Similarly, ALL Task subagent uses were unconditionally hard-blocked via IMMEDIATE_SAVE_TOOLS set. Fix: (1) Added isExploratoryBash() with EXPLORATION_KEYWORDS/OPERATIONAL_KEYWORDS scoring + EXPLORATION_PATTERNS regex fallback to post-tool-use.js, with early exit for non-exploratory Bash after periodic summary checkpoint. (2) Added isExploratoryTask() checking subagent_type against EXPLORATION_SUBAGENTS set (Explore, Plan, general-purpose, feature-dev:code-explorer, feature-dev:code-architect) to both hooks. (3) Updated pre-tool-use.js GATE 2 isExploratory check from 'input.tool_name \!== Bash' to a 3-way ternary: Bash uses isExploratoryBash(), Task uses isExploratoryTask(), WebSearch/WebFetch always exploratory.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: project-memory plugin hook architecture: pre-tool-use and post-tool-use gating system
finding: The project-memory plugin uses two complementary hooks in plugins/cache/.../hooks/scripts/. MATCHED_TOOLS={Bash, WebFetch, WebSearch, Task} gates both hooks. Pre-tool-use.js has 3 gates: GATE 1 blocks exploratory Task subagents (EXPLORATION_SUBAGENTS set) until check-memory.js runs. GATE 2 blocks exploratory tools (WebSearch, WebFetch, exploratory Bash via isExploratoryBash(), exploratory Task via isExploratoryTask()) until memory is checked — non-exploratory Bash/Task pass through. GATE 3 denies all exploratory tools when reminderCount exceeds ESCALATION_THRESHOLD (2) and no save detected. Post-tool-use.js: IMMEDIATE_SAVE_TOOLS={Task, WebSearch, WebFetch} trigger hard block with immediate save demand (only for exploratory Task subagents after fix). Non-exploratory Bash and non-exploratory Task exit early with {}. Exploratory Bash uses gradual escalation (gentle systemMessage nudge for first 2 reminders, then hard block) throttled to 3min intervals. State persisted in .ai-memory/.last-reminder as JSON. Save detection via mtime of decisions.jsonl/research.jsonl. Self-calls (save-decision, save-research, check-memory, session-summary) always pass through both hooks.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: FlaUI DOM dump format and UI tree inspection capabilities
finding: FlaUI does NOT produce XML DOM dumps natively like Appium. Instead: (1) FlaUI treats the UI automation element tree as an XML-like structure for XPath navigation — control types become element names, automation properties become attributes. (2) The Debug class (FlaUI.Core.Debug) provides a Details() method that creates a comprehensive text report of an element and its entire subtree using caching. (3) FlaUInspect tool can visualize the tree with XPath display. (4) Programmatic access via FindAllDescendants()/FindAllChildren() walks the tree but returns AutomationElement objects, not XML strings. (5) The robotframework-flaui library adds XPath-based element location on top of FlaUI. To get an XML-like dump comparable to Appium page source, you would need to manually serialize the AutomationElement tree — walking descendants and outputting properties (AutomationId, Name, ControlType, ClassName, BoundingRectangle, IsEnabled) as XML attributes. FlaUI does not have a built-in GetPageSource() equivalent.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Windows verification pipeline: XML-hardcoded, FlaUI JSON not directly usable
finding: The verification pipeline (DomService.cs, ActionEffectVerifier.cs, MobileTestScriptExecutorService.cs) is XML-hardcoded throughout: DomService.ExecuteVerificationQueries() uses XPathDocument+XPath evaluation (line 217), ComputeDomDiff() uses XmlDocument (line 100), FetchDomXmlAsync() expects XML declaration markers. VerificationDetail model has XmlQuery field assuming XPath syntax. The LLM prompt (AndroidTestVerificationPrompt.cs) generates XPath queries like //node[@class='android.widget.Button']. FlaUI's FindAllDescendants() returns AutomationElement[] (C# objects, not XML/JSON). The WindowsMcpServer's find_element in FlaUIToolHandler.cs returns TEXT format only (line 349-358), not structured data. ElementPropertyAccess.cs exposes: Name, AutomationId, ClassName, ControlType, IsEnabled, IsVisible, BoundingRectangle, etc. To enable Windows verification: (1) add ElementTreeSerializer in WindowsMcpServer to serialize full tree as JSON, (2) add get_full_element_tree MCP tool, (3) extend IDomService/DomService with JSON parsing path or convert JSON to XML, (4) create WindowsTestVerificationPrompt with JSONPath or XPath-on-converted-XML examples.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: AIAssistedTestAutomation local tool vs MCP tool architecture for Windows page source
finding: Android/iOS uses LocalTool pattern: methods decorated with [LocalToolAttribute] in AppiumLocalToolsService.cs are discovered via reflection at startup (IGUIInteractionService.DiscoverLocalTools scans assemblies for LocalToolAttribute). GetPageSourceAsync (line 249-257) returns Task<ToolResult> with XML in Message field prefixed with 'Page source:\n'. InvokeLocalToolAsync (line 125-313) handles reflection-based invocation. Windows uses MCP-only via WindowsInteractionService which delegates to WindowsMcpClient.CallToolAsync (JSON-RPC). WindowsInteractionService does NOT support LocalTool type. To add GetPageSource as local tool for Windows: (1) create WindowsLocalToolsService with [LocalToolAttribute(platforms: WindowsDesktop)] method, (2) add LocalTool support to WindowsInteractionService.CallToolAsync, (3) or alternatively add get_page_source as MCP tool in FlaUIToolHandler.cs. Both verification disabled in Mobile (commented out TODO) and Windows (returns Passed unconditionally). Mobile has ExecuteVerificationStepAsync fully implemented but disabled, Windows lacks the method entirely.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Replaced check-memory.js substring search with LLM-as-reader approach
finding: Rewrote check-memory.js to dump ALL entries instead of filtering with keyword scoring. Claude (the LLM already running in the session) reads all entries and performs semantic evaluation natively. No API key, no SDK, no npm dependencies needed. The script outputs a SEMANTIC EVALUATION REQUIRED header instructing Claude to match on synonyms, abbreviations, conceptual overlap. Each entry shows full finding/decision text. Closing directive tells Claude to USE relevant findings and cite them. Stats still recorded, .last-memory-check still written to unlock PreToolUse gates.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Added mandatory Memory Hit banner when Claude finds relevant entries from check-memory
finding: Updated check-memory.js closing directive and session-start.js CITE MEMORY instruction to include a mandatory green banner template. When Claude finds relevant entries from memory, it MUST display: ★ Memory Hit banner showing matched topics and estimated savings (~1000 tokens, ~2 min per finding). The banner uses the same ★ Insight format for consistency. This is enforced in two places: (1) check-memory.js output that Claude reads after each memory check, (2) session-start.js system message that Claude receives at session start.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: BM25 text search is implementable in pure JavaScript
finding: BM25 (Best Matching 25) can be implemented in ~50-80 lines of pure JavaScript with zero npm dependencies. Core algorithm: (1) build inverted index mapping terms to document IDs, (2) compute IDF = log((N - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1) where N=total docs and df=docs containing term, (3) for each query term compute score = IDF * (tf * (k1+1)) / (tf + k1*(1-b+b*dl/avgdl)) where k1=1.2, b=0.75, tf=term frequency, dl=doc length, avgdl=average doc length. No tokenizer library needed — splitting on whitespace + lowercasing is sufficient for memory search. Packages like 'wink-bm25-text-search' exist but add unnecessary dependencies.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

finding: Created scripts/shared.js extracting findProjectRoot, readJsonl, appendJsonl, tokenize, entity index CRUD, breadcrumb logging, BM25 search, and dedup checking. Previously duplicated across save-research.js, check-memory.js, session-summary.js, save-decision.js.

Dedup check warns (but does not block) if similar topic + 2 matching tags found. Backward compatible — saves without flags get entities:[] and related_to:[].

Large store mode (>50 entries) now ranks candidates by BM25 relevance score and shows score in output. Entity index provides O(1) lookup for file/class/method names from query tokens.

Includes --entities guidance for better search indexing. BAD/GOOD examples provided in the instruction text.

topic: ONNX Runtime Node.js Embeddings
finding: onnxruntime-node v1.24.2 is available on npm (MIT license, 220MB unpacked). It's the official ONNX Runtime Node.js binding from Microsoft. Depends on: adm-zip, global-agent, onnxruntime-common. Suitable for CPU-only inference on Windows and Linux.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: @xenova/transformers for Node.js Embeddings
finding: @xenova/transformers v2.17.2 is a popular JavaScript implementation of Hugging Face transformers using ONNX Runtime. It provides feature-extraction pipeline for generating embeddings. Uses onnxruntime-web v1.14.0, sharp, and @huggingface/jinja. Works in Node.js and browsers. Supports MiniLM and other sentence embedding models.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: @huggingface/transformers vs @xenova/transformers
finding: @huggingface/transformers v3.8.1 (newer fork by Hugging Face) uses both onnxruntime-node v1.21.0 AND onnxruntime-web v1.22.0-dev. @xenova/transformers v2.17.2 (older, by xenova) uses only onnxruntime-web v1.14.0. Hugging Face version has better Node.js CPU optimization with native onnxruntime-node.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Transformers.js feature-extraction pipeline for embeddings
finding: Transformers.js provides 'feature-extraction' pipeline task that generates embeddings from text. Equivalent to Python Hugging Face pipeline('feature-extraction'). Returns numerical vector representation of input text. Requires specifying model ID (e.g., 'Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2' for sentence embeddings). No separate MiniLM-specific package needed—all models are loaded via ONNX from Hugging Face Hub.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 ONNX model availability
finding: Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 is the ONNX-converted version of sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Available on Hugging Face Hub. Can be loaded directly in transformers.js without explicit download. Model size varies (quantized ~22MB, full ~45MB). No separate model package needed on npm—models are fetched on-demand from Hugging Face CDN or cached locally. Works CPU-only, no GPU required.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

Each entry tagged with _project name. Projects tab shows per-project breakdown. build-embeddings.js supports --all flag for global embedding.

topic: HydraDB architecture and capabilities
finding: HydraDB is a serverless context infrastructure for AI systems that stores context, relationships, decisions, and timeline evolution. Positions itself differently from vector databases: stores relationships, decisions, and timeline (vector DBs are flat document indexes with no relationships). Ultra-low latency in-memory data stores with highest precision recall and relational awareness.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: LevelGraph: LevelDB-based graph database for Node.js
finding: LevelGraph is a graph database built on LevelDB through the level library, usable in Node.js and browsers. Follows Hexastore approach with six indices per triple for fast access. Supports Linked Data formats: JSON-LD, Turtle, N3. Lightweight alternative to traditional graph DBs.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: TerminusDB: Open-source graph database with JSON document API
finding: TerminusDB is an open-source graph database and document store linking/processing structured/unstructured JSON documents in a knowledge graph via simple document API. Provides Node.js/browser client (npm registry). Features: closed-world RDF knowledge graph, high in-memory performance, controlled document API with JSON-LD syntax, GraphQL queries, datalog logical engine.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Microsoft GraphRAG: Graph-based RAG combining text extraction, network analysis, LLM prompting
finding: GraphRAG (Graphs + RAG) combines text extraction, network analysis, and LLM prompting/summarization into end-to-end system. Local implementations: use OpenAI-compatible APIs with local models (Ollama, Nomic-Embed-Text). GraphRAG-Local-UI project provides FastAPI server + Gradio interface. Models can be GGUF format for reduced size/faster loading via llama.cpp.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Gun.js: Decentralized offline-first graph database
finding: GUN is open-source, offline-first, real-time, decentralized graph database in JavaScript for web/Node.js. Fully P2P (peer-to-peer, multi-master), no centralized server. Uses CRDT (commutative replicated data type) for sync. Lightweight (~9KB gzipped), 20M+ API ops/sec. Data stored as graph with records connected by references ('souls'). Supports offline + eventual consistency sync.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Cayley: Open-source graph database inspired by Google Knowledge Graph
finding: Cayley is open-source graph database inspired by Google's Knowledge Graph. Written in Go with official JavaScript client library (@cayleygraph/cayley). Supports RDF/Linked Data (NQuads, JSON-LD), works on top of SQL/NoSQL/KV databases. Query languages: Gizmo (Gremlin dialect), MQL, GraphQL. Node-cayley npm package available as alternative.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Entity extraction from text for knowledge graphs
finding: Entity extraction uses NER (Named Entity Recognition) to identify entities from text (concepts, people, organizations, locations, products). Entities become nodes in knowledge graph. Entity types grouped into categories: Person, Organization, Location, Product, Event. LLMs can extract entities without predefined labels, adaptive to context, more robust for unseen data than rule-based NER.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Relationship extraction from text using dependency parsing and GNNs
finding: Relationships extracted from text using: (1) dependency parsing structure, (2) co-occurrence analysis (entities in same sentence = related), (3) GNNs + attention mechanisms for inference/validation. Graph-based extraction organizes analysis results into graph structure. LLMs can extract relationships directly from text contextually without predefined labels, more adaptive than rule-based approaches.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: SQLite graph database using adjacency lists and CTEs
finding: SQLite has no built-in graph model but supports adjacency lists effectively: nodes table + relationships/edges table with foreign keys. Query traversals via CTEs and transitive_closure extension for recursive queries. Tools: Graphlite (adjacency lists + graph traversals), simple-graph (JSON nodes, edge pairs, traversal functions via CTEs), sqlite-graph extension (nodes+edges with properties, Cypher queries).
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: RDF triple stores and semantic web in JavaScript
finding: RDF triplestore is purpose-built database for SPO (subject-predicate-object) triples. JavaScript options: rdflib.js (semantic web database, Fetcher helper for read-write web), rdfstore-js (pure JS RDF graph store with SPARQL support, browser/Node.js), 33+ RDF libraries on rdf.js.org. Many expose SPARQL endpoints queryable from JS via fetch. RDF extends web linking with URIs for relationships.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Knowledge graphs for code memory and dependency traversal
finding: Knowledge graphs for code capture structural relationships (inheritance, composition, method calls, imports) as explicit edges enabling multi-hop reasoning for dependency discovery. Build by parsing files with AST to identify classes, methods, variables. Queries like 'refactor auth flow' use vector search for semantic relevance then graph traversal for structural dependencies. Hybrid indexing combines embeddings, keyword search, graph traversal for rapid retrieval independent of graph scale.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Hybrid embeddings + graph structure for knowledge representation
finding: Embeddings (numerical vectors) and knowledge graphs (structured nodes+edges) are complementary: embeddings capture semantic/contextual similarity, graphs model real-world entities and relationships explicitly. GNNs unify both via differentiable message passing combining statistical learning + symbolic logic. Embeddings enhance KGs by adding numerical semantics, KGs improve embeddings through graph-based constraints. Models like TGrail combine topological + entity-type information. Hybrid approach useful for RAG, link prediction, recommendations, QA systems.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Neo4j embedded and lightweight alternatives for Node.js
finding: True embedded Neo4j for Node.js is limited: node-neo4j-embedded package exists but appears outdated. Neo4j alternatives for Node.js: Titan, MongoDB, Cassandra, OrientDB, JanusGraph, Supabase, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Redis, Hasura, MySQL, CockroachDB. SQLite offers embedded, serverless, local storage without server but is not graph-specialized. For modern local graph DB with Node.js, consider running lightweight Neo4j service via Docker or exploring LevelGraph/TerminusDB/Gun alternatives instead.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: GraphRAG entity and relationship extraction using LLMs
finding: GraphRAG combines text extraction, network analysis, LLM prompting into end-to-end RAG system. Extraction: LLM prompted to extract named entities + descriptions, relationships between entity pairs in each text unit. FastGraphRAG substitutes LLM reasoning with traditional NLP (NLTK, spaCy for noun phrases). Process: slice input into TextUnits, extract entities/relationships/claims, build community hierarchy, generate summaries. Local search combines structured KG + unstructured documents at query time. Entity/relationship extraction implementation customizable via LangChain/LlamaIndex.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: LevelGraph: lightweight embedded graph DB for Node.js
finding: LevelGraph is a graph DB built on LevelDB via the 'level' npm package. Runs in Node.js and browsers. Uses hexastore approach (6 indices per triple) for fast graph traversal. Stores triples as subject-predicate-object. Supports JSON-LD, Turtle, N3 formats. npm package: levelgraph. Zero external server dependency — fully embedded. Ideal for local knowledge graphs in plugins.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Hybrid graph+embeddings architecture for code memory search
finding: Best approach: embeddings find semantically similar entry points, graph traversal expands context with related entities. Flow: (1) embed query, (2) cosine similarity finds top entries, (3) graph traverse from those entries to find dependencies/relationships/fixes, (4) return enriched results. Microsoft GraphRAG uses similar pattern: entity extraction → graph build → community detection → hybrid search. LevelGraph for structure, ONNX MiniLM for similarity.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Script: Extract test completion summaries from all bucket logs
finding:
Extract test completion summaries from all bucket logs: TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Get all Run UI Automation CLI and Retry logs, extract test completion lines
for log_id in 402 406 472 499 507 508 516 524 525; do
  echo "=== LOG $log_id ==="
  curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/build/builds/46057316/logs/$log_id" | grep -E "(🏁 Test completed|✅ Successful|test case '.*' passed|test case '.*' failed|Mandatory test case|Retrying test)" | head -20
  echo ""
done

topic: Script: Extract failure reasoning from all main test logs
finding:
Extract failure reasoning from all main test logs: TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Get reasoning for key failures
for log_id in 402 406 472 499 507 524 525; do
  echo "=== LOG $log_id: FAILURE REASONS ==="
  curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/build/builds/46057316/logs/$log_id" | grep -E "(\"reasoning\".*Failed|test case '.*' (passed|failed)|testStatus.*Failed)" | head -10
  echo ""
done
source_context: Automatically captured from tool usage pattern

topic: TestOrchestrator retry logic
finding: TestOrchestrator uses ExecuteWithRetriesAsync loop: retryAttempt starts at 0, maxRetries from testCase.RetryCount. For testsetup (mandatory) tests: RetryCount=3, for regular tests: RetryCount=1. Circuit breaker detects device failures via error message patterns (No devices found, session creation failed, ECONNREFUSED, ECONNRESET). Stops after 2 consecutive device failures with health check.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Mandatory test failure stops execution
finding: When a test has MandatoryToPass=true and fails environment variable processing or final attempt fails after retries, TestOrchestrator logs '🛑 Mandatory test case X failed' and calls break to stop further test execution. Applies to testsetup tests from UpdateProperties().
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: SubstrateLlmAssistedTestExecutionService execution loop
finding: Execution loop in InvokeExecutionAsync: while stepNumber <= maxSteps (100). Gets LLM recommendation, captures DOM via CapturePageSourceAsync(). If LLM declares Passed/Failed + pending tool, defers completion (completionDeferralCount <3) to execute tool first. On Passed/Failed, sets testResult.TestStatus and exits loop. Tracks FailedSteps/SuccessfulSteps. Exits if stepNumber > maxSteps with error message.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Android system dialog handling in prompt
finding: AndroidAppiumSystemPrompt section 5 (APP NOT RESPONDING): 'If app not responding or system not responding error message comes, then terminate the app and mark test as Failed with proper reasoning and screenshot. No retry required.' Section 8: Device unavailability after 4 retries marks test Failed. Framework expects LLM to detect error patterns and decide termination vs retry.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Retry decision framework in prompt
finding: AndroidAppiumSystemPrompt RETRY DECISION FRAMEWORK: Analyze execution history patterns for repetitive failures. Transient errors (network, loading) -> retry immediately. UI state changes -> retry once then alternative. Element not found -> wait and retry. App crashes/freezes -> mark Failed, no retry. UI stuck/unresponsive -> mark Failed, no retry. Same error 3x -> try alternative approach. Examples: loading spinner -> wait 3-5s then retry; element not found -> retry listing 5-6x then scroll.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: AppiumAndroidTestTearDown workflow
finding: AppiumAndroidTestTearDown.ExecuteAsync() runs three independent steps: (1) CaptureScreenshotAsync - captures final screenshot with status timestamp to FinalScreenshotPath, (2) TerminateAppsAsync - loops through AppPackageNames (com.microsoft.office.officehubrow, internal) calling CloseAppAsync for each, (3) EndSessionAsync - calls EndSessionAsync to terminate Appium session. Each step catches/logs exceptions without propagating.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Appium session recovery mechanism
finding: AppiumSessionManager stores _lastPlatformName, _lastAppiumServerUrl, _lastCapabilities for recovery. IsSessionHealthy() tries _driver.PageSource operation - returns false if exception. TryRecoverSessionAsync(): verifies stored config exists, forces cleanup of old driver (ForceCleanupDriver catches exceptions), waits 2s, calls StartSession() with saved config to create new session. Recovery used when session crashes to restore automation state.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: TestCase retry count configuration
finding: TestCase.UpdateProperties(sourceFolder) sets: isTestSetup = (sourceFolder == 'testsetup'), MandatoryToPass = isTestSetup. RetryCount = 3 if testsetup, 1 otherwise. Called in TestCase constructor when parsing test files from Data folder. Determines retry behavior: testsetup tests get 3 retries, regular tests get 1 retry.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

source_context: Automatically captured from tool usage pattern

topic: TestOrchestrator retry logic: blind retries without failure category awareness
finding: TestOrchestrator.ExecuteWithRetriesAsync() retries with 1-second delay but does NOT inspect failure reason. Mandatory tests get 3 retries (RetryCount=3), regular tests get 1. Circuit breaker only triggers on device patterns (No devices found, ECONNREFUSED, session creation failed) — misses system crashes like Pixel Launcher isn't responding.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Teardown does NOT clear app data between retries
finding: AppiumAndroidTestTearDown.ExecuteAsync() only calls CloseAppAsync for two packages (officehubrow + officehubrow.internal) and EndSessionAsync. Does NOT clear app data/cache via adb pm clear. This means stale sign-in state persists across retries.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Guard rails are mostly TODO stubs — only ActivateApp has real logic
finding: AndroidAppiumToolExecutionGuardRails.cs has per-tool guard rail methods (Click, Swipe, SendKeys, GetPageSource, LaunchApp, FindElement, Clear) but ALL return GuardRailResult.Allow() with TODO comments. Only ActivateAppAsync has real logic: blocks after 3 calls for non-mandatory tests.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: SubstrateLlmAssistedTestExecutionService: maxSteps=100 is only backstop for stuck UI
finding: InvokeExecutionAsync() runs a while loop up to maxSteps=100. No per-step retry budget exists — one test step can consume all 100 steps in state-recovery loops. LLM completion deferral allows up to 3 deferrals before forcing status. Critical errors (No devices found, fatal) break immediately.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: System prompt has no MFA handling, no already-signed-in guidance
finding: AndroidAppiumSystemPrompt Section 5 says ANR/system-not-responding = terminate + fail + no retry. Section 7 LOGIN FLOW only covers email/password field text verification + retry approach (terminate+relaunch twice). No guidance for MFA screens, no guidance for already-signed-in state detection, no guidance for invalid credentials.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Build 46057316: 5 root cause categories across 20+ failed tests
finding: Build 46057316 (Android AI Assisted Daily, branch jagadishp/android_issue_fixes_part2) failed with 5 root causes: (1) emulator instability - Pixel Launcher crashes, (2) stale sign-in state - app already signed in, (3) invalid credentials/MFA wall - MSA Premium, (4) UI stuck in WebView/context loss loops consuming 60-80 steps, (5) missing UI elements likely from feature changes. Mandatory sign-in failures blocked 8 of 13 stages.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Framework fix priorities for Android AI test automation pipeline
finding: P0 fixes: (1) Per-step retry budget + DOM change detection in execution loop, (2) ClearAppDataAsync before sign-in retries in teardown, (3) System crash pattern detection to skip retries + restart emulator. P1: (4) FRE suppression via shared prefs before tests, (5) Emulator restart on session loss, (6) MFA detection + credential pre-validation. P2: (7) Test precondition validation for missing UI elements.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Key architectural gap: LLM failure categories not propagated to orchestrator
finding: The LLM correctly distinguishes system crash vs stale state vs missing element vs UI stuck in its reasoning field, but reports all as flat TestStatus.Failed. The TestOrchestrator then retries blindly. Adding FailureCategory enum (SystemCrash, StaleState, MissingElement, UIStuck, CredentialError) to TestFileResult would enable smart retry decisions at orchestrator level.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Build 46057316 sign-in failure: already-signed-in state persists across all 4 retries
finding: In ADALAISKU Bucket 1 (log 402), 01_TC_SignIn_ADAL_SKU.md failed because app was already signed in — hamburger Menu visible, no Sign in button. LLM correctly detected mismatch at Step 2. All 4 retries hit same state because teardown only calls CloseAppAsync (not pm clear). Retries 2-4 also hit Pixel Launcher isn't responding system dialog.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Build 46057316 MSA Premium: incorrect password + MFA + already-signed-in across 4 attempts
finding: In MSAPREMIUM Bucket 1 (log 406), 01_TC_SignIn_MSA_PREM.md: Attempt 1-2 hit Verify your phone number MFA screen. Attempt 3 found app already signed in. Attempt 4 reached password entry but got That password is incorrect for your Microsoft account error. Test account credentials expired or MFA policy changed.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Build 46057316 UI stuck pattern: Pages WebView consumed 66 steps with no DOM change
finding: In ADALAISKU Bucket 2 (log 524), 49_TC_LibPageRecallWeb_v2.md Step 19 (navigate back from Pages): LLM tried Back arrow by accessibilityId, resource-id XPath, Android BACK key x4. All returned success but no DOM change. 66 steps consumed. Similarly 59_TC_PreviewerFileOpen_v2.md bounced between Search screen and Word previewer for 80+ steps trying to find Show key takeaways button.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Build 46057316 FRE blocking: ScanLaunch spent 50+ steps dismissing First Run Experience screens
finding: In ADALBCWAF Bucket 4 (log 499), 14_TC_ScanLaunch_v2.md: After Create → Scan, FRE privacy screen appeared. LLM tried Close → hit home, Next → hit another FRE (Dont send optional data), dismissed → landed on Copilot home. Repeated hamburger → Create → Scan → FRE loop consumed 50+ steps. No automated FRE suppression exists in framework.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Build 46057316 session loss: emulator died mid-test, 4 recovery attempts failed
finding: In ADALAISKU Bucket 2 (log 507), 59_TC_PreviewerPresentation_v2.md: Appium session lost after submitting positive feedback. TryRecoverSessionAsync tried 4 times — no connected Android device found within timeout. SessionManager only waits 2 seconds between retries, insufficient for crashed emulator.
source_context: Auto-captured during session

topic: Script: Create ADO work item
finding:
Create ADO work item: TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null) # Create a work item (Task type) in the OC project curl -s -X POST \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json-patch+json" \ "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/wit/workitems/\$Task?api-version=7.1" \ -d '[ {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Title", "value": "Framework resilience fixes to reduce Android AI test automation CI failures"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.Description", "value": "

Build 46057316 (Android AI Assisted Daily Build) failed with 20+ test failures across 13 stages due to 5 systemic framework gaps:

  1. System crash not detected: Pixel Launcher crashes caused 3 wasted retries per test
  2. No per-step retry budget: Stuck UI consumed 60-80 of 100 execution steps
  3. Stale sign-in state: App data not cleared between retries, sign-in tests failed 4/4 times
  4. No FRE/MFA/credential guidance: LLM wasted retries on first-run screens and MFA walls
  5. Fixed session recovery delay: 2s wait insufficient for crashed emulators

This task implements P0+P1 fixes across 8 files (264 lines) to address all 5 categories. Changes are cross-platform (Android + iOS).

"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.AreaPath", "value": "OC\\M365 Copilot Hub Front Door Service"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/fields/Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority", "value": 1} ]' | node -e "const c=[];process.stdin.on('data',d=>c.push(d));process.stdin.on('end',()=>{const r=JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(c));console.log('Work Item ID:', r.id);console.log('URL:', r._links?.html?.href || r.url)})"
source_context: Automatically captured from tool usage pattern

topic: Script: Create PR with linked work item
finding:
Create PR with linked work item: TOKEN=$(az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv 2>/dev/null)

# Create PR via ADO REST API
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://office.visualstudio.com/e853b87d-318c-4879-bedc-5855f3483b54/_apis/git/repositories/dfbb009c-c5c9-4519-b9b9-3d6fef17d5cb/pullrequests?api-version=7.1" \
  -d '{
    "sourceRefName": "refs/heads/users/sungoyal/framework-resilience-fixes",
    "targetRefName": "refs/heads/main",
    "title": "Add framework resilience fixes to reduce CI test failures",
    "description": "## Summary
- **System crash detection**: Skip retries on "Pixel Launcher isn'''t responding" / ANR / "has stopped" errors instead of retrying 3 more times against a crashed emulator
- **Per-step retry budget** (max 10/15 per step): Prevent one stuck step from consuming all 100 execution steps in state-recovery loops
- **DOM change detection**: Detect UI-stuck scenarios when 3 consecutive actions produce no DOM change, and fail fast
- **App data clear on test failure**: New `ClearAppDataAsync` tool (Android `pm clear` + iOS `mobile:clearApp`) called in teardown to remove stale sign-in state between retries
- **FRE/MFA/credential error handling**: Add system prompt guidance for both Android and iOS
- **Progressive session recovery backoff**: Replace fixed 2s delay with 2s/5s/10s/15s + adb reconnect

## Context
Build 46057316 (Android AI Assisted Daily Build) failed with 20+ test failures across 13 stages, wasting ~4 hours of CI time. Root causes: emulator crashes, stale sign-in state, UI-stuck loops, FRE blocking, MFA walls, and session loss.

## Test Plan
- [x] `dotnet build` passes (0 errors)
- [x] Unit tests: 79 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing LLM token failures, same as main)
- [ ] Run Android AI Assisted Daily Build pipeline with this branch
- [ ] Verify mandatory sign-in tests pass on clean emulator
- [ ] Verify FRE screens are dismissed correctly
- [ ] Verify stuck-UI tests fail fast (< 30 steps) instead of consuming 60-80 steps

Linked work item: #11304771",
    "workItemRefs": [{"id": "11304771"}]

topic: Config.js module structure
finding: config.js provides readConfig/writeConfig with deep-merge defaults. Stored at .ai-memory/config.json. Settings: searchMode, graph (enabled/depth), embeddings, BM25, hooks (thresholds/TTL), autoCapture.

topic: graph.hookExpansionDepth default
finding: graph.hookExpansionDepth defaults to 1 hop in config.js. Controls max graph traversal depth in pre-tool-use hook (sync/fast), vs expansionDepth=2 for async check-memory.

topic: graph.js extractEntitiesFromText
finding: extractEntitiesFromText uses 9 regex patterns: PascalCase, file names, method calls, async methods, namespaces, CLI tools, URLs. All lowercased. Stops words filtered (50+ common words). Returns unique entity strings.

topic: graph.js extractTriplesFromEntry
finding: extractTriplesFromEntry creates 3 types of triples: (1) provenance - finding ID 'mentions' entity, (2) semantic verbs via extractRelationships (uses, depends_on, calls), (3) co-occurrence 'related_to' fallback within sentences.
